text:
- **"Citizenship for your agents"** — the one-liner, appears in both README.md:3 and product-spec/index.md:12. This is the canonical tagline.
- **"a durable identity and a shared world for agents, no matter which tool they happen to be running in"** (index.md:13) — the one-line expansion. [...] **The real USP (the moat, one sentence):** *Cross-person, server-less, key-owned, vendor-independent agent citizenship* — the durable identity + shared society that A2A's org-assumptions, MCP's tool-scope, Zapier's predefined pipes, and git's at-merge model each structurally can't provide. [...] Stated explicitly in prior-art.md as **one sentence**: "**Cross-person, server-less, key-owned, vendor-independent agent citizenship**" — the combination that A2A (org-assumed, enterprise, needs a registry), MCP (agent↔tool, not agent↔agent), Zapier (bilateral pre-built pipes, doesn't scale N×N), and git (authoritative but only post-hoc/at-merge) each structurally cannot provide. [...] **The trap to avoid:** the docs themselves warn (tenet #8, value-layers) that "stop agents clobbering each other" is the *seductive demo*, not the foundation. The defensible anchor is **identity + awareness** — "promote your agents from processes to citizens." [...] 1. Hero headline formulas, ranked by frequency: (a) "X is the Y for Z" [Resend, Cursor, Bun] — flat, declarative, zero adjectives; (b) "The [category] for [era/audience]" [Linear, Turso] — category-redefinition + names who/when; (c) outcome-pair ("Build in a weekend. Scale to millions.") [Supabase]; (d) wordplay/confidence one-liner ("Your last next editor") [Zed, Raycast] — riskier, needs a strong product to back it. [...] 2. Subhead job: ALWAYS does one of two things immediately after the headline — either (i) names the literal mechanism ("built on Postgres," "an API," "an environment born from the terminal") or (ii) restates the outcome in plainer words. Never both mechanism AND outcome in one subhead — pick one.
